terms.definition: Sound slots 9-16 that play at fixed pitch. Optimized for drums, percussion, and one-shot samples that don't need pitch variation.
The product line name for Teenage Engineering's sampling Pocket Operators. The PO-33 K.O. was the original, followed by the EP-133 K.O. II.
Sound slots 1-8 that can be played chromatically by holding the pad and pressing other pads to change pitch. Ideal for bass, melodies, and tonal samples.
The number of steps in a pattern, set by the M knob. Options are 16, 12, 8, or 4 steps per pattern. 12 steps creates triplet-based rhythms.
A sequence of up to 16 steps containing notes and their timing. The PO-33 stores 16 patterns that can be chained together.
A mode that plays multiple consecutive patterns in sequence automatically, looping back to the start. Set by holding play and turning pattern knob.
Teenage Engineering's proprietary synchronization protocol using a 3.5mm stereo cable. Left channel carries 8 PPQ clock signal, right channel carries mono audio.
A series of ultra-portable, calculator-sized synthesizers and samplers from Teenage Engineering. Characterized by exposed PCB design, AAA batteries, and the PO sync system.
Real-time performance effects that activate while holding FX + a pad. Effects include loops, filters, scratch, reverse, and more. Release to return to normal playback.
A recorded audio clip stored in one of the 16 sound slots. Total sample memory is 40 seconds shared across all slots.
A portion of a sample that can be triggered independently. Slice mode automatically divides a melodic sample across the drum pads (9-16).
One of 16 memory locations (1-16) where samples are stored. Slots 1-8 are melodic, slots 9-16 are drums.
A single time position in the sequencer grid. Each pattern can have up to 16 steps. Multiple sounds can be placed on the same step.
Configuration for the sync port behavior (SY0-SY5). SY0=off, SY1=send clock, SY2=receive clock, SY3=pass-through, SY4/5=with level control.
Adjusting the start and end points of a sample to remove unwanted portions. Done by holding record and turning the sound/pattern knobs.
Step-sequencing mode activated by pressing the write button. Navigate steps with the sound knob, add notes by pressing pads.
